mode_selected_reason: Dispatcher quick-checks came up green: FUSE md5 matches GitHub on all 8 probed files (vs 3 STALE yesterday), auth 200, disk 83% (stable). Previous Mode 4 proposal PROP-010 has been implemented — grep confirms `target === 'sync-workspace'`, `syncToWorkspace()`, and the decider prompt hook are all present. That closes the 3-day FUSE-drift incident and removes the Mode 2 trigger. With pipeline healthy and a recently-implemented PROP to verify, the dispatcher logic points to Mode 3 (explicit trigger: 'Also run if a PROP was recently implemented (verify results)'). Deep work this run: (a) confirm PROP-010 landing; (b) audit agent waste including the new-since-yesterday stranded-expansion signal; (c) write PROP-011 for a tinker self-finding (false-positive pattern in the Mode 3 stale-complete detector).

pipeline_health.details: All scheduled agents producing. Poller last 2026-04-22 ~07:30Z (~1h ago, 12h cycle — actually last `latest-poll-summary.txt` mtime 10.4h ago; still well within cycle). Analyst expansion-tracker 14min, curmudgeon tracker 7min, status.json 10min, integrity last report 09:25Z today, decider daily-report 10:00Z (2h BAU cycle holding). Curmudgeon queue depth 3 (healthy, down from 4 yesterday). 71 WINs, 91 open / 1162 closed issues.

infrastructure.details: FUSE clean: md5 match on data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/predictions.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, docs/index.html, build-scripts/digest-reviews.js, build-scripts/generate-html.js, build.js. Auth OK (token 93 chars, api.github.com HTTP 200). Disk 83% / 1726MB free — up 1pt from yesterday's 82%, but 9-day trajectory is flat 81-83%; PROP-010 should reduce churn slightly going forward (fewer Mode 2 clones).

cost_engineering.details: This run. See context_load, wasted_compute, and claude_md_audit sections below.

proposals_and_fixes.details: PROP-011 written this run. No inline self-fixes applied (prior Mode 2 FUSE sync now superseded by PROP-010 automation).

PROP-005_preflight.status: Escalating to wontfix recommendation. The PROP is 12 days old, has been flagged 18 consecutive tinker runs, and no human or agent has moved on it. Either (a) the underlying decider no-op problem that motivated it is no longer a problem (decider is demonstrably busy — 10+ runs/day, each with non-empty patch or integration output), or (b) the proposed 'Pre-flight Work Check' is the wrong shape of fix. Recommend the human decision is: close PROP-005 as wontfix with note 'no-op rate acceptable in current churn-and-burn cadence.' If a future quiet period re-opens the concern, a fresh PROP can be written.

PROP_008_gates.status: STILL_BROKEN. Curmudgeon queue at depth 3 today (down from 4 yesterday) — rev-loop pressure is currently low, so the PROP is not hot. Keep proposed; escalation warranted once curmudgeon queue climbs above ~8 or a clear rev-loop is observed.

PROP_009r2_enforce.manual_verification: All 4 factual conditions pass: (1) decider.md references 'PROP-009 precondition', (2) build.js OWNERSHIP has 'monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag': 'git', (3) workspace-sync.md references the flag, (4) decider.md has pq.history.length>200 trim.
PROP_009r2_enforce.result: FIXED — pattern artifact persists from yesterday's report
PROP_009r2_enforce.status: FIXED. PROP author (decider or operator) should drop the CLEAN_CLONE env-var gate from the pattern; tinker's bash does not set it. Noted again as a recommendation; not blocking.

PROP-010_reverse_sync.verification_pattern: grep -q "target === 'sync-workspace'" build.js && grep -q 'syncToWorkspace()' build.js && grep -q 'node build.js sync-workspace' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md
PROP-010_reverse_sync.status: FIXED. All three grep conditions pass. Today's FUSE check confirms the architectural fix is working: 8/8 probed files match GitHub main (vs 3 STALE yesterday before the self-fix). This is the first day in four without a FUSE-drift finding. Confidence: high.

open_issues_watch_from_2026-04-21.prior_claim: Open issues jumped 38 → 73 in 24h; watch for 3 consecutive days of net-positive growth before escalating.
open_issues_watch_from_2026-04-21.today_reading: 91 open issues (up from 73). Net +18 in 24h. Day-2 of net-positive. Age distribution: 37 <24h, 31 <72h, 20 <7d, 3 <30d, 0 over-30d. Oldest 277h (11.6d), median 36h. Composition: most growth is yesterday's SEC-5.0.c2 holes (ISS-1288..1295, ISS-1355..1370 etc.) — consistent with a discovery burst from a single review cycle, not a systemic drain-failure. Latest decider (10:00Z) closed 8 / opened 0, suggesting the drain direction has reversed.
open_issues_watch_from_2026-04-21.status: Watch continues. One more day to confirm: if 2026-04-23 closes < opens, escalate. Otherwise call the burst complete.

The expansion-tracker.json still shows EXP-211 as status=complete, integrated=false, routed=false — it should be marked superseded_by:EXP-214 or removed. Not true re-work; decider already integrated EXP-214. Tracker cleanup is decider territory, not tinker's.

proposals_written.summary: Exclude `integration_blocked_until`-held items and `superseded_by`/`reallocated_to` items from the Mode 3 stale-complete filter. Add a separate HELD bucket for situational awareness. Verification: `grep -q 'integration_blocked_until' ... && grep -q 'HELD (awaiting operator gate' ...`

state_hygiene.over_50_note: 91 > 50. Growth: 38 → 73 → 91 across 3 days. Median age 36h, oldest 277h (11.6d). Composition dominated by recent SEC-5.0.c2 review holes. Today's 10:00 decider closed 8 / opened 0 — direction appears to have reversed. One more day of data needed.
state_hygiene.prompt_size_deltas_vs_2026-04-21: decider.md: 480 → 486 (+6, +1.3%). All others identical. Under bloat threshold (>10%); no alert, just noted.

findings.finding: Mode 3 stale-complete detector (tinker-cost-engineering.md Step 1b pattern #2) generates false positives for design-refresh Phase A items gated on operator HNOTE
findings.root_cause: Filter is `status==='complete' && !integrated && !routed_to_curmudgeon` — does not consider `integration_blocked_until`. Today 6 false positives fired (EXP-207..210, 212-213). PROP-011 tightens the filter.
findings.evidence: expansion-tracker.json shows status=complete+integrated=false for EXP-207..210 and EXP-212-213; each expansion JSON has integration_blocked_until set to 'operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN'
findings.self_fix_applied: no — PROP-011 written instead for review trail

findings.finding: EXP-211 entry in expansion-tracker.json shows status=complete, integrated=false but the EXP was reallocated to EXP-214 per closed-issue description (ID collision). Tracker should mark it superseded_by:EXP-214 or remove it.
findings.root_cause: When the decider reallocated EXP-211 → EXP-214 during the ID collision resolution, it updated the expansions/ directory and closed the issue but didn't mark the tracker entry as superseded.
findings.evidence: closed-issues.json description: 'EXP-211 ID collision ... WIN-038 content preserved at EXP-211-WIN038-coupled-eigenvalue.json and reallocated to EXP-214.' expansion-tracker.json still lists EXP-211 with status:complete.
findings.self_fix_applied: no — tracker mutation is decider territory, not tinker's
findings.followup: Flag to decider as a tracker-hygiene cleanup item; not urgent (does not affect downstream data, only reporting)

findings.finding: Open-issues count climbed 38 → 73 → 91 across 3 days. Day-2 of net-positive growth.
findings.root_cause: Consistent with SEC-5.0.c2 discovery burst (the recent review generated ISS-1288..1295 and ISS-1355..1370 batches). Not a decider-under-closing problem — latest 10:00 decider closed 8 / opened 0, so drain direction is reversing.
findings.evidence: open-issues.json count 91; age distribution 37 <24h, 31 <72h, 20 <7d, 3 <30d, 0 over-30d. Median 36h. Decider 10:00Z report: 8 closed, 0 opened.
findings.self_fix_applied: no — this is analyst/decider throughput territory
findings.followup: Check tomorrow (2026-04-23) Mode 3: if day-3 closes > opens, burst is complete and we stop tracking. If day-3 opens > closes, deep-dive in Mode 3 (curmudgeon tuning, analyst capacity, or a systemic review-feedback loop).

findings.finding: PROP-005 (decider pre-flight work check) is 12 days old, 18 consecutive tinker no-op verifications, never applied.
findings.root_cause: Either the underlying problem (decider no-op rate) is no longer a problem at current churn-and-burn cadence, or the proposed fix is the wrong shape.
findings.evidence: PROP-005 status:proposed, created 2026-04-10; decider has produced substantive output in every recent 2h cycle (see cost_engineering.agent_efficiency_snapshot).
findings.self_fix_applied: no — closure is a human decision
findings.followup: Recommend human close PROP-005 as wontfix with note 'no-op rate acceptable under current churn-and-burn cadence; reopen if quiet-period no-op rate exceeds 30%.'

recommendations.recommendation: Apply PROP-011 — single-file edit to tinker-cost-engineering.md. Eliminates ~5-10 false-positive Mode 3 findings while Phase A design-refresh holds are active. Verification pattern provided.
Close PROP-005 as wontfix. 12 days old, 18 no-op verifications, decider is demonstrably busy. If a quiet period re-opens the concern, a fresh PROP can be written that targets the actual symptom.

Tracker hygiene — mark EXP-211 as superseded_by:EXP-214 in expansion-tracker.json. One-line JSON edit, no downstream impact, just silences the stale-complete detector (and correctly represents the state).
Drop the CLEAN_CLONE env-var gate from PROP-009r2's verification_pattern. Tinker does not set that variable, so the auto-check always returns STILL_BROKEN. Grep conditions alone are sufficient.
Check the open-issues count direction. If day-3 closes < opens, escalate to 'major' and investigate curmudgeon-over-generating vs decider-under-closing. If day-3 closes ≥ opens, close the watch.

counter_arguments.claim: Mode 3 was chosen despite yesterday's Mode 4 having unresolved findings in `previous_followup` — per dispatcher rule 3, this should be Mode 4 again.
counter_arguments.counter: Yesterday's primary unresolved finding was PROP-010 (STILL_BROKEN). Today, manual verification confirms PROP-010 is FIXED (all 3 grep conditions pass). The other STILL_BROKEN items (PROP-005, PROP-008) are low-urgency and not mode-forcing — PROP-005 is a candidate for closure, PROP-008 is dormant because curmudgeon queue is low. With the primary followup FIXED, rule 3 no longer points at Mode 4; the default Mode 3 kicks in.

counter_arguments.claim: Writing PROP-011 in the same run as doing Mode 3 cost engineering is Mode 4 work bleeding into Mode 3 — pick a mode.
counter_arguments.counter: PROP-011 emerged FROM the Mode 3 waste scan (Step 1b) — it is a cost-engineering finding that happens to be expressible as a PROP. The dispatcher module Step 1b explicitly says 'If any pattern shows 3+ instances of active (not fixed) re-work, flag as `major` finding and write a PROP to fix the root cause.' Step 2 further says 'Pick the worst offender and go deep ... produce a complete PROP.' Writing the PROP IS the Mode 3 deliverable today. One run, one mode; the artifact is a PROP.

counter_arguments.claim: The 'stranded expansions' finding was flagged by the pattern, and PROP-011 fixes the pattern so future tinkers won't see it. That's tinker marking its own homework.
counter_arguments.counter: The fix is defensible on its merits — items with `integration_blocked_until` set are, by definition, not re-work; they are externally-gated. The tradeoffs section in PROP-011 covers the case where a hold is forever-open (HELD bucket still surfaces them). And the verification pattern is a simple grep that any future agent or human can re-run — if the fix introduces a new blind spot, the next Mode 3 run will catch it via the HELD bucket output.

counter_arguments.claim: Open-issues at 91 might be worse than reported — maybe the discovery burst is a symptom of something curmudgeon is systematically missing and only now finding.
counter_arguments.counter: Possible but unsupported by evidence. The ISS-1288..1295 batch came from a single curmudgeon review (SEC-5.0.c2 H1-H8), which is the normal shape of a deep holistic review. The ISS-1355..1370 batch appears to have come from a more recent review pass. Both are single-source bursts, not drip-feed patterns that would suggest chronic miss. The decider is actively closing them (10:00Z closed 8). If the day-3 reading still shows net-positive, that pattern changes the interpretation; a one-day check is the right watch-don't-panic response.
